topics.name: 递归函数基础
topics.subject: computer_science

prerequisites.reason: Master 函数与参数传递 before 递归函数基础; it provides the foundation used by later steps.
Recursive correctness arguments need a clear base case and induction-style step; proof-writing structure helps students justify the recursion, not just run it.

related.reason: 递归函数基础 and 函数与参数传递 are often studied together, so guidance can compare their conditions and usage.
Practice 指针与地址 after 递归函数基础; it is the next topic in this course sequence.
Recursive functions implement recurrence-style definitions, so sequence recursion gives a mathematical model for base cases and recursive steps.
Recursive functions often generate ordered terms, so sequence concepts help students name each call result as a term indexed by depth.
Divide-and-branch recursion can create term counts that grow like a geometric sequence across recursion levels.
After writing a recursive base case and recursive step, students can justify correctness with an induction-style proof structure.

Finding a bug at a boundary is often confused with designing full test coverage.

topics.name: 算法复杂度分析
topics.subject: computer_science

prerequisites.reason: Master C 程序边界调试 before 算法复杂度分析; it provides the foundation used by later steps.
Asymptotic complexity compares algorithms as functions of input size, so function thinking is needed before interpreting growth rates.

related.reason: Sorting practice uses time-complexity analysis to compare algorithms as input size changes.
Complexity analysis first counts primitive operations, then applies the result to linear-list access, insertion, and deletion costs.
算法复杂度分析 and C 程序边界调试 are often studied together, so guidance can compare their conditions and usage.
Practice 线性表顺序存储 after 算法复杂度分析; it is the next topic in this course sequence.
Exponential growth such as 2^n must be distinguished from polynomial or logarithmic complexity when ranking algorithms.
Logarithmic functions explain why halving-search patterns such as binary search have much slower growth than linear scans.
Polynomial complexity classes such as n, n^2, and n^3 are easier to compare when students read them as power functions.
Repeated halving in algorithms maps directly to logarithm operations when solving for the number of steps.
Runtime analysis builds a function model T(n) from input size to operation count before comparing algorithms.
Asymptotic notation compares long-run growth, so the limit concept supports explanations of dominant terms and negligible constants.

Adjacency lists and adjacency matrices encode vertices, edges, direction, and weights from graph theory.

related.reason: BFS and DFS depend on the graph representation to enumerate neighbors accurately.
Graph storage turns vertex and edge sets into linear structures that can enumerate adjacent vertices.
图的存储结构 and 堆与优先队列 are often studied together, so guidance can compare their conditions and usage.
Practice 图的 BFS 与 DFS after 图的存储结构; it is the next topic in this course sequence.
An adjacency matrix is a matrix representation of a graph; matrix operations can express reachability and weighted-edge calculations.
An adjacency matrix for n vertices has n by n dimensions, which connects graph storage choices to matrix shape.
Weighted graph representations store the edge weights that shortest-path models use as path lengths.

prerequisites.reason: Master 图的存储结构 before 图的 BFS 与 DFS; it provides the foundation used by later steps.
BFS and DFS are algorithmic procedures for the mathematical idea of graph traversal.

related.reason: Topological sort applies graph traversal ideas to directed acyclic graphs with in-degree updates.
Graph traversal first chooses BFS or DFS, then uses a queue or stack to manage pending vertices.
图的 BFS 与 DFS and 图的存储结构 are often studied together, so guidance can compare their conditions and usage.

Traversal is commonly used to test connected components and prove reachability in graph models.
BFS traversal gives shortest paths in an unweighted graph because it visits vertices by increasing edge distance.
DFS and BFS can record parent links that form traversal trees, which connect directly to spanning-tree ideas.
When solving graph traversal problems, the code step of marking visited vertices mirrors the mathematical traversal process.
